title: MongoDB Capped Collections - Complete Guide (Beginner to Advanced)
description: Comprehensive guide on capped collections for fixed-size, circular buffer collections

capped_collection_overview.definition: Capped collection is a fixed-size collection that automatically removes oldest documents when size limit exceeded
capped_collection_overview.concept: Circular buffer - when full, oldest documents deleted to make room for new ones
key_characteristics: Cannot be modified (documents cannot be updated)
capped_collection_overview.note: Must be created with capped option; cannot convert regular collection to capped

section_1_beginner_concept.title: BEGINNER: Understanding Capped Collections
section_1_beginner_concept.definition: Fixed-size collection with automatic oldest-document removal when limit reached
characteristics: Size limit: Collection won't exceed specified size
Count limit: Optional maximum document count
Automatic deletion: Oldest documents deleted when limit exceeded
No updates: Cannot modify documents (can only insert/delete)
No indexes: Cannot create or delete indexes after creation
No sharding: Cannot be sharded across servers
when_not_to_use: Need document count to grow unbounded

section_2_beginner_creation.title: BEGINNER: Creating Capped Collections
section_2_beginner_creation.definition: Capped collections must be explicitly created with capped option
section_2_beginner_creation.syntax: db.createCollection('name', { capped: true, size: bytes, max: docCount })

beginner_examples.title: Create capped collection for activity log
beginner_examples.behavior: When 1001st document inserted, oldest document deleted

beginner_examples.title: Create size-only capped collection
beginner_examples.behavior: When 5 MB exceeded, oldest documents deleted

beginner_examples.title: Create count-only capped collection
beginner_examples.note: When max not specified, must specify size
beginner_examples.issue: Both size and max optional, but at least one required

important_notes: Size in bytes, not MB (1MB = 1048576 bytes)
Both size and max optional, but at least one required
Actual storage may be slightly more than specified size

beginner_examples.title: Automatic oldest deletion
beginner_examples.query: db.studentActivity.insertOne({ studentId: '1', action: 'login', time: new Date() })
beginner_examples.step3: Insert doc3: collection has [doc1, doc2, doc3]
beginner_examples.step4: Insert doc4: collection has [doc2, doc3, doc4] (doc1 auto-deleted)

beginner_examples.title: Size-based deletion
beginner_examples.insertion: When 101st document inserted, oldest deleted automatically

beginner_examples.title: Insertion order preserved
beginner_examples.behavior: Documents returned in insertion order by default
beginner_examples.result: Returns documents in insertion order (oldest first)

intermediate_examples.title: Bulk insert into capped collection
intermediate_examples.behavior: All inserted; oldest removed if limit exceeded

intermediate_examples.title: Delete all documents (clear collection)
intermediate_examples.note: This is allowed; clears but doesn't delete collection

section_5_intermediate_vs_ttl.title: INTERMEDIATE: Capped vs TTL Indexes
section_5_intermediate_vs_ttl.definition: Two ways to automatically delete documents

intermediate_examples.title: Preallocated space
intermediate_examples.concept: Capped collection preallocates space on disk
intermediate_examples.cost: Space used even if not full

intermediate_examples.title: Conversion to capped (workaround)
intermediate_examples.workaround: Create new capped, copy data, drop old, rename
intermediate_examples.query:
db.createCollection('newCapped', { capped: true, size: ... })
db.oldCollection.find().forEach(doc => db.newCapped.insertOne(doc))

advanced_examples.title: Optimize for query performance
advanced_examples.note: Cannot add indexes, so design carefully

advanced_examples.title: Replication with capped
advanced_examples.caveat: Each replica is separate capped collection

intermediate.note: Consider TTL index instead for time-based expiration

queries.optimization: Full collection scan for non-_id queries

practice_1_size_calculation.why: Buffer for overhead, prevents unexpected deletions

practice_2_use_max_too.rule: Specify both size AND max when possible
practice_2_use_max_too.benefit: Double safety - whichever limit hit first

practice_3_plan_before_creation.rule: Plan indexes and queries before creating

practice_4_monitor_space.rule: Monitor collection to ensure size sufficient

practice_5_consider_ttl_alternative.rule: Use TTL if time-based expiration needed
practice_5_consider_ttl_alternative.when: Want to keep X days of data

mistake_1_conversion.solution: Create new capped, copy data, rename

mistake_3_update_attempt.problem: Error: cannot update in capped collection
mistake_3_update_attempt.solution: Delete and re-insert if change needed

mistake_4_index_after_creation.mistake: Try to add index after creation
mistake_4_index_after_creation.solution: Plan before creating, recreate if needed

mistake_6_wrong_use_case.solution: Use capped only for temporary/log data

summary.core_concept: Fixed-size collection with automatic oldest-document removal
summary.creation_syntax: db.createCollection('name', { capped: true, size: bytes, max: count })
summary.best_practice: Calculate size carefully, specify both size and max, plan queries before creation
summary.progression: Beginner: Concepts and creation → Intermediate: Behavior and alternatives → Advanced: Optimization and monitoring
